pr.title: Split some files out of text_2.s
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Moved three ground-library assembly slices out of the monolithic asm/melee/text_2.s into named files under asm/melee/gr: grlib.s, grdynamicattr.s, and grzakogenerator.s. The PR body states the split was based on original C filenames found in assert strings. obj_files.mk was updated so the new asm objects build in place of the removed text_2.s ranges. No review discussion was present.

key_files.role: Source monolithic assembly file; about 1975 lines were removed and replaced by small residual changes.
New split assembly file for grlib.c-derived functions and data; added about 786 lines.
New split assembly file for grdynamicattr.c-derived functions and data; added about 253 lines.
New split assembly file for grzakogenerator.c-derived functions and data; added about 906 lines.
Build list updated to include the three new asm object files after text_2.s.o and before existing gr asm objects.

what_changed: Created asm/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.s and moved functions beginning around func_801CA0B4 plus associated labels, assert strings, bss, sbss, and sdata2 constants into it.
Created asm/melee/gr/grlib.s and moved functions beginning around func_801C96E8 plus its data/constants into it.
Created asm/melee/gr/grzakogenerator.s and moved the corresponding text_2.s assembly block plus its original filename assert string into it.
Removed the extracted assembly blocks from asm/melee/text_2.s.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grlib.s.o, grdynamicattr.s.o, and grzakogenerator.s.o.
smart_moves: Used embedded assert filename strings, including "grdynamicattr.c", "grlib.c", and "grzakogenerator.c", as direct evidence for original source file boundaries.
Preserved assembly labels and global symbol names while moving code, reducing risk of introducing matching or relocation changes during a pure file split.
Moved each file's related .text, .data, .bss, .sbss, and .sdata2 fragments together, which helps avoid separating code from local constants or assert strings.
Updated the object list rather than leaving the new files unreferenced, making the split immediately build-relevant.
decomp_lessons: Assert strings are strong evidence for original C file names and can justify splitting monolithic asm files before functions are fully decompiled.
When extracting from text_2.s, carry nearby rodata/data/bss/sbss/sdata2 labels with the function group if they are referenced by the moved code.
File-boundary recovery can be a useful prerequisite for later C matching because it creates smaller, semantically named targets under the expected subsystem directory.
For assembly-only splits, avoid unnecessary symbol renaming; preserve func_ and lbl_ identifiers until stronger type/function evidence is available.
Build-order edits in obj_files.mk are part of the match: new object files should be placed where the extracted ranges originally appeared in link order.
naming_conventions: Recovered file basenames followed embedded assert source names: grlib, grdynamicattr, and grzakogenerator.
Ground subsystem assembly files were placed under asm/melee/gr/ with lowercase filenames matching the inferred original C basenames.
Unidentified functions retained func_80xxxxxx names and unidentified labels retained lbl_80xxxxxx/lbl_804xxxxx names.
The PR did not introduce higher-level C symbol names; evidence only supports file-level naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split monolithic asm by original filename markers found in .asciz assert strings.
Preserved .include "macros.inc" and section structure in each new .s file.
Kept address-based function labels and branch labels unchanged across the move.
Included non-text sections in the new files where needed: .bss, .data, .sbss, and .sdata2.
Adjusted obj_files.mk so the linker sees the new gr asm objects in the intended sequence after text_2.s.o.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No reviewer-stated rationale beyond the author's note about assert-derived filenames was available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile grlib.s into C.
Find later PRs that decompile grdynamicattr.s or name its data structures.
Find later PRs touching grzakogenerator.s after the file split.
Search for other text_2.s splits based on embedded assert filename strings.
Check whether the obj_files.mk ordering around text_2.s.o and asm/melee/gr/*.s.o remained stable in subsequent PRs.
